items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (Multiple Vulnerabilities)
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload
items.summary: A critical unrestricted file upload vulnerability (CVSS 9.6) addressed in APSB26-73, which could lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Improper Output Encoding
items.summary: A critical improper output encoding vulnerability (CVSS 9.1) resolved in APSB26-73, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.summary: A critical stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.7) addressed in APSB26-73, which could lead to unauthorized actions or data access.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVSS 8.6) fixed in APSB26-73, potentially allowing unauthorized access to restricted data or actions without authentication.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization / Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
items.summary: Adobe Commerce versions 2.4.9-beta1 and earlier are affected by a high-severity Incorrect Authorization vulnerability that could lead to security feature bypass and unauthorized write access. It also includes an SSRF vulnerability that could result in unauthorized read access.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered the critical PolyShell vulnerability, a flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (Multiple Vulnerabilities)
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: A critical vulnerability resolved in APSB26-05 that could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: Another critical arbitrary code execution vulnerability addressed in APSB26-05.

items.title: Security Feature Bypass
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in APSB26-05 that could allow an attacker to bypass security features.

items.title: Privilege Escalation
items.summary: A critical privilege escalation vulnerability resolved as part of APSB26-05.

items.title: Arbitrary File System Read
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in APSB26-05 that could lead to arbitrary file system read access.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Unauthenticated)
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVSS 8.2) in APSB26-73 that requires no authentication, potentially allowing anonymous visitors to perform unauthorized actions.
